hypothesis: If a stereochemistry-aware diffusion model (LoQI) is trained on a large dataset of neural-network-potential-optimized conformers (ChEMBL3D), then it will generate low-energy molecular conformers with near-quantum-mechanical accuracy that are competitive with or superior to classical conformer generation methods across diverse drug-like molecules, including those with complex stereochemistry and high flexibility, because explicit stereochemical graph augmentations and large-scale high-quality training data enable the model to learn the geometric and energetic principles governing stable molecular conformations.
